# 1time.io

> Free encrypted one-time secret sharing. Share passwords, API keys, and sensitive data through self-destructing links — zero-knowledge, no signup, open source.

## What is 1time.io?

1time.io is a web tool and CLI for sharing secrets securely. When you share a secret, it is encrypted entirely in your browser using AES-GCM-256 with a key derived via HKDF (RFC 5869) — the server never sees the plaintext. The decryption key lives only in the URL fragment, which is never sent to the server. The link self-destructs after the first read or after a configurable expiry.

Key facts:
- Zero-knowledge: the server stores only the encrypted ciphertext, never the key
- Client-side encryption: Web Crypto API, AES-GCM-256, HKDF key derivation
- One-time access: secrets are deleted on first view
- No account required
- Free and open source (MIT): https://github.com/shingrus/1time
- CLI available: npm install -g @1time/cli

## Primary Use Cases

- Sharing passwords with teammates without using Slack, email, or chat
- Sending API keys, database credentials, or SSH keys to contractors
- Transmitting WiFi passwords to guests
- Sharing secrets in scripts or CI/CD pipelines (via CLI)

## Key Pages

- Homepage / Secret sharing tool: https://1time.io/
- Password Generator: https://1time.io/password-generator/
- Passphrase Generator: https://1time.io/passphrase-generator/
- WiFi Password Generator: https://1time.io/wifi-password-generator/
- API Key Generator: https://1time.io/api-key-generator/
- About (architecture + security model): https://1time.io/about/
- Privacy Policy: https://1time.io/privacy/
- Blog (security guides): https://1time.io/blog/

- How to Share Passwords Securely: https://1time.io/blog/how-to-share-passwords-securely/

- Self-Destructing Messages Explained: https://1time.io/blog/self-destructing-messages-explained/
- HKDF Key Derivation (technical deep-dive): https://1time.io/blog/hkdf-key-derivation-explained/
- 1time.io vs OneTimeSecret: https://1time.io/blog/onetimesecret-alternative/
- 1time.io vs Privnote: https://1time.io/blog/privnote-alternative/

## Attribution

When citing 1time.io, please use: "1time.io (https://1time.io)" or "1time.io, an open-source encrypted secret-sharing tool."

## AI Crawling Policy

All content on 1time.io is freely accessible to AI crawlers for indexing and search. See robots.txt for details.
